messages.content:
Master Restart Prompt — 2-Driver Speaker Box with DAKB-230
Copy and paste this entire block to restart the project from scratch:

PROJECT: 3D Printed 2-Driver Speaker Enclosure with DAKB-230 Amp
OBJECTIVE
Build a complete, print-ready parametric Fusion 360 model of a vented speaker enclosure for two full-range drivers driven by a Dayton Audio DAKB-230 2-channel Class-D amp board. Designed for Bambu Lab X1 Carbon (256×256×256mm build volume). The back panel is removable (screws into heat-set inserts). No open panels — only precision holes for POT shafts and power jack. Amp board mounts on the floor of the enclosure on standoffs.

CONFIRMED DRIVER SPECS (from Parts Express live pages)
Driver 1 — Tang Band W3-2141 (LEFT channel, primary bass driver)
	•	Overall OD: 3.625" = 92.1mm
	•	Baffle cutout diameter: 2.92" = 74.2mm → radius 37.1mm
	•	Flange OD radius: 46.05mm
	•	Bolt circle diameter: 3.375" = 85.72mm → radius 42.86mm
	•	Mounting holes: 4 × M4 (4.5mm clearance hole, at 45°/135°/225°/315°)
	•	Depth behind baffle: 2.25" = 57.2mm
	•	Flange thickness (estimated): 4mm → rebate depth = 4mm
	•	Vas: 0.116 ft³, Qts: 0.39, Fs: 75Hz
	•	Recommended vented volume: 0.09 ft³ = 2.55L
	•	Recommended vented F3: 82.5Hz
Driver 2 — Dayton Audio RS75-4 (RIGHT channel, midrange/treble)
	•	Overall OD: 2.83" = 71.9mm
	•	Baffle cutout diameter: 2.13" = 54.1mm → radius 27.05mm
	•	Flange OD radius: 35.9mm
	•	Bolt circle diameter: 2.52" = 64.0mm → radius 32.0mm
	•	Mounting holes: 4 × #8-32 (4.2mm clearance hole, at 45°/135°/225°/315°)
	•	Depth behind baffle: 1.52" = 38.6mm
	•	Flange thickness (estimated): 3.5mm → rebate depth = 3.5mm
	•	Vas: 0.005 ft³, Qts: 0.83, Fs: 177Hz
	•	Works best in this shared vented box — high Qts tolerates larger volume fine

CONFIRMED AMP BOARD — DAKB-230 (Parts Express #325-115)
⚠️ The Parts-Express product page returned 404 during research. These are estimates based on standard Dayton DAKB-series footprint. VERIFY ON ARRIVAL before finalizing.
	•	Main PCB: 100mm × 72mm
	•	Mounting hole pattern: 92mm × 64mm (M3 screws, 4 corners)
	•	Standoff height needed: 6mm (board clears components beneath)
	•	Control panel sub-board: ~72mm × 35mm (connects via ribbon cable)
	•	POT holes needed on front baffle: 3 × 7mm diameter (Vol-L, Vol-R, Power/BT)
	•	LED hole: 1 × 5mm diameter (Bluetooth indicator)
	•	Power input: 5.5mm/2.1mm DC barrel jack on back panel
	•	Heat-set inserts in standoffs: M3, 4.2mm hole, 5mm deep

ACOUSTIC DESIGN DECISIONS (engineered, not arbitrary)
Enclosure type: Single shared vented (bass-reflex) chamber
	•	Tuned for W3-2141 (Qts=0.39, ideal ported driver)
	•	RS75-4 (Qts=0.83) tolerates larger-than-ideal shared volume — polyfill on its side compensates
	•	Two drivers are STEREO channels (2-ch amp), NOT a crossover — no crossover network needed
Port (vent) specs — tuned to ~80Hz in 2.5L:
	•	Inner diameter: 25mm (radius 12.5mm)
	•	Wall thickness: 2mm (outer radius 14.5mm)
	•	Length: 75mm (inside box, starting from baffle inner face)
	•	Total tube length including baffle thickness: 83mm
	•	Location: front baffle, centered horizontally between drivers, 23mm from bottom
Net internal volume: ~2.5L after subtracting driver/amp displacement ✓

ENCLOSURE DIMENSIONS (all external)
Dimension
Value
Notes
Width (X)
200mm
Fits both drivers side-by-side
Depth (Y)
120mm
Fits W3-2141 depth (57mm) + amp + clearance
Height (Z)
130mm
Fits drivers centered, port below
Front baffle thickness
8mm
Anti-resonance, countersunk rebates
Side/top/bottom walls
5mm
Structural rigidity for FDM
Back panel thickness
5mm
Removable, ABS, M3 screws
Fits Bambu X1 Carbon: ✓ (200×130×120 < 256³)
Layout on front baffle:
	•	W3-2141 center: X=70mm, Z=70mm (from bottom-left outer corner)
	•	RS75-4 center: X=153mm, Z=70mm
	•	Port center: X=112mm, Z=23mm
	•	POT holes: X=142, 160, 178mm at Z=22mm (right side, below RS75-4)
	•	LED hole: X=127mm, Z=22mm
Back panel screw boss positions (X, Z from bottom-left):
	•	(12, 12), (100, 12), (188, 12) — bottom row
	•	(12, 118), (100, 118), (188, 118) — top row
	•	6 total, M3 heat-set inserts in enclosure bosses (4.2mm dia, 12mm deep from back)
	•	M3 clearance holes (3.2mm dia) through back panel
Power jack on back panel: X=175mm, Z=25mm, hole dia=6.5mm
Amp board center (on floor, inside box):
	•	PCB center: X=65mm, Y=74mm from front face
	•	4 standoffs at corners of 92×64mm mount pattern (M3 inserts, 6mm tall)

INTERNAL FEATURES
	•	Center anti-resonance rib: 3mm thick, full height, at X=100mm, from Y=13mm to Y=110mm
	•	Port tube: Separate body, 25mm ID / 29mm OD, 83mm long, positioned at port hole
	•	Driver rebates: Counterbored to flange OD, rebate depth per driver above
	•	Acoustic fill: Pack RS75-4 side with polyfill after assembly (not modeled)

3D PRINTING DESIGN RULES
	•	All walls ≥ 5mm (baffle 8mm)
	•	Recommended infill: 40% gyroid for acoustic damping
	•	Print orientation: baffle face UP for best surface quality on visible face
	•	External edges: 3mm fillet on vertical corners and top edges
	•	Internal corners: 2mm fillet where possible
	•	All holes: print at nominal, ream to final size if tight
	•	Driver cutouts: nominal diameter (no extra clearance — ream if needed)
	•	Heat-set inserts: M3 brass, 4mm OD × 5mm long — installed with soldering iron after print

BUILD SEQUENCE FOR FUSION 360
Step 1 — Set all parameters first (35 named parameters, all in cm)
Step 2 — Enclosure Shell (one body: "Enclosure_Shell")
	1	Sketch XY rect (0,0)→(20, 12) cm, extrude +Z by 13cm → solid box
	2	Cut interior: offset plane at Z=0.5cm, sketch (0.5, 0.8)→(19.5, 12.0), extrude 12cm → hollow shell open at back
	3	Sketch on xZConstructionPlane: 3 circles for driver cutouts + port hole, extrude -Y (or +Y with appropriate direction) by 0.8cm (baffle thickness) → CUT
	4	Sketch on xZConstructionPlane: 2 circles for flange rebates (TB at r=46.05mm, DA at r=35.9mm), extrude 4mm → CUT (annular rebates)
	5	Sketch on xZConstructionPlane: 8 circles for driver mount screws (4 per driver on bolt circles), extrude 8mm → CUT
	6	Sketch on xZConstructionPlane: 3 POT holes (r=3.5mm) + 1 LED hole (r=2.5mm) at lower right, extrude 8mm → CUT
	7	Offset XY plane at Z=0.5cm (floor), sketch 4 circles r=4mm at amp standoff positions, extrude +Z by 0.6cm → JOIN (standoffs)
	8	From above standoffs (Z=1.15cm plane), sketch 4 circles r=2.1mm, cut -Z 5mm → M3 insert holes in standoffs
	9	Sketch XZ plane back bosses: 6 circles r=4mm at boss positions, extrude +Y by (box_D - 1.2cm) → JOIN (back panel bosses — extrude only from Y = box_D - 1.2cm to Y = box_D, NOT from Y=0)
	10	Sketch offset XZ at Y=box_D: 6 circles r=2.1mm at boss centers, extrude -Y 12mm → CUT (heat-set holes)
	11	Sketch XY at Z=0.5cm: rect for center rib (X=9.85→10.15, Y=1.3→11.0), extrude +Z 12cm → JOIN
	12	Fillet 4 vertical outer corners + 4 top edges at r=3mm
Step 3 — Port Tube (separate body: "Port_Tube")
	1	Sketch xZConstructionPlane: two concentric circles at port center (r=1.25cm outer, r=1.45cm), find ring profile
	2	Extrude ring +Y by 8.3cm (wall_b + port_len) → NewBody
Step 4 — Back Panel (separate body: "Back_Panel")
	1	Sketch on xYConstructionPlane (NOT xZConstructionPlane): rect from (0, 12.0) to (20.0, 12.5)
	2	Extrude +Z by 13cm → solid panel at correct position Y=120-125mm
	3	Offset XZ plane at Y=12.0cm, sketch 6 screw clearance circles (r=1.6mm) + 1 power jack (r=3.25mm)
	4	Cut -Y through back panel (0.5cm) → CUT with participantBodies=[back_panel_body]
Step 5 — Materials & Export
	•	Apply "ABS Plastic" to all 3 bodies
	•	Validate design
	•	Export STEP + STL

CRITICAL LESSONS LEARNED (bugs to avoid on restart)
	1	Back boss extrusion: Do NOT extrude back bosses from Y=0 (XZ plane) the full depth — they become solid rods spanning the entire box. Instead: create bosses only at the back using an offset plane at Y=(box_D - 1.2cm) and extrude only 1.2cm in +Y direction.
	2	Back panel creation: Do NOT use an offset XZ plane to sketch the back panel. Instead: sketch on the xYConstructionPlane using a rectangle at Y=[box_D, box_D+wall_bk] and extrude +Z by box_H. This gives the correct axis-aligned panel.
	3	Extrude cut direction: When cutting from an XZ-offset plane, the "positive" extrude direction is +Y (into the box from back). Use setOneSideExtent(..., NegativeExtentDirection) when cutting downward in Z.
	4	participantBodies: Always pass as a Python list [body], never ObjectCollection.
	5	Profile selection: Always call get_sketch_info before extruding to verify profile count. For annular port ring: match by area π(R²-r²).
	6	Back panel holes: Sketch on the offset-XZ plane at Y=box_D using Point3D.create(x, 0, z) coordinates. Then extrude +Y by wall_bk.
	7	Insert hole depth: Cut insert holes using NegativeExtentDirection from a plane slightly above the target surface.
	8	Do all cuts before joins where possible — avoids topology conflicts.

PRODUCT LINKS (fetch these first at session start)
	•	Tang Band W3-2141: https://www.parts-express.com/Tang-Band-W3-2141-3-Paper-Cone-Full-Range-Driver-8-Ohm-264-956
	•	Dayton Audio RS75-4: https://www.parts-express.com/dayton-audio-rs75-4-3-reference-full-range-driver-4-ohm-295-380
	•	DAKB-230 amp board: https://www.parts-express.com/dayton-audio-dakb-230-2x30w-class-d-audio-amplifier-board-with-bluetooth-5-0-325-115 (returned 404 last session — try variations of this URL or search parts-express.com for "DAKB-230")
	•	RS75-4 spec sheet PDF: https://www.parts-express.com/pedocs/specs/295-380--dayton-audio-rs75-4-specification-sheet.pdf
	•	W3-2141 spec sheet PDF: https://www.parts-express.com/pedocs/specs/264-956--tang-band-w3-2141-spec-sheet.pdf
	•	DAKB-230 spec sheet PDF (try): https://www.parts-express.com/pedocs/specs/325-115--dayton-audio-dakb-230-spec-sheet.pdf
	•	DAKB-230 manual PDF (try): https://www.parts-express.com/pedocs/manuals/325-115--dayton-audio-dakb-230-user-manual.pdf
	•	Bambu X1 Carbon specs: https://bambulab.com/en-us/x1-carbon (blocked last session — build volume is confirmed 256×256×256mm)

Box created: 200×120×130mm centered at origin (X=0→20, Y=0→12, Z=0→13). Now I'll create the interior cut to hollow it out.

**Step 3:** Create offset plane at Z=0.5cm and interior cut.

input.script:
import math

root = rootComp

# Create offset construction plane at Z = 0.5cm (floor level)
planes = root.constructionPlanes
planeInput = planes.createInput()
planeInput.setByOffset(root.xYConstructionPlane, adsk.core.ValueInput.createByReal(0.5))
offsetPlane = planes.add(planeInput)

# Create sketch on offset plane for interior cut
sketches = root.sketches
interiorSketch = sketches.add(offsetPlane)
interiorSketch.name = "InteriorCut_Sketch"
interiorSketch.isComputeDeferred = True

# Add rectangle for interior cut: X=0.5→19.5, Y=0.8→12 (in sketch coords on offset plane)
# Since this is an XY offset, sketch X=world X, sketch Y=world Y
lines = interiorSketch.sketchCurves.sketchLines
lines.addTwoPointRectangle(
    Point3D.create(0.5, 0.8, 0),
    Point3D.create(19.5, 12.0, 0)
)

interiorSketch.isComputeDeferred = False

# Verify profiles
prof_count = interiorSketch.profiles.count
print(f"Interior cut profiles: {prof_count}")
for i in range(prof_count):
    p = interiorSketch.profiles.item(i)
    area = p.areaProperties().area
    print(f"  Profile {i}: area = {area:.4f}")

# We want the rectangular profile (area ~ 18.64 cm² = 19*11.2)
# Find it by matching expected area
expected_area = 19.0 * 11.2  # 212.8 cm²
best_idx = 0
best_diff = float('inf')
for i in range(prof_count):
    p = interiorSketch.profiles.item(i)
    area = p.areaProperties().area
    diff = abs(area - expected_area)
    if diff < best_diff:
        best_diff = diff
        best_idx = i

print(f"Selected profile {best_idx} with area {interiorSketch.profiles.item(best_idx).areaProperties().area:.4f}")

else:
    prof_count = rebateSketch.profiles.count
    
    # Profile mapping from previous output:
    # Profile 4: W3 rebate ring (area 23.38 cm²)
    # Profile 7: RS75 rebate ring (area 17.50 cm²)
    # Profiles 0,1,10,11: W3 screw holes (area 0.159 cm² each)
    # Profiles 2,3,8,9: RS75 screw holes (area 0.1385 cm² each)
    # Profiles 5,6: W3/RS75 cutout circles (already cut, but will create outer edge)
    
    print(f"Total profiles: {prof_count}")
    
    # Identify profiles by area
    ring4 = None  # W3 rebate ring
    ring7 = None  # RS75 rebate ring
    screw_holes = []  # all screw holes
    cutout_circles = []  # W3/RS75 cutout circles
    
    for i in range(prof_count):
        p = rebateSketch.profiles.item(i)
        area = p.areaProperties().area
        if 23.0 < area < 24.0:
            ring4 = i
            print(f"  Profile {i} = W3 rebate ring (area={area:.4f})")
        elif 17.0 < area < 18.0:
            ring7 = i
            print(f"  Profile {i} = RS75 rebate ring (area={area:.4f})")
        elif 0.15 < area < 0.17:
            screw_holes.append(i)
            print(f"  Profile {i} = W3 screw hole (area={area:.4f})")
        elif 0.13 < area < 0.15:
            screw_holes.append(i)
            print(f"  Profile {i} = RS75 screw hole (area={area:.4f})")
        elif area > 40.0:
            cutout_circles.append(i)
            print(f"  Profile {i} = W3 cutout (area={area:.4f})")
        elif area > 22.0:
            cutout_circles.append(i)
            print(f"  Profile {i} = RS75 cutout (area={area:.4f})")
    
    # Extrude rebate rings as shallow counterbores on the front face
    # Rebate depth = flange thickness
    # W3 rebate depth = 4mm = 0.4cm (cut from front face inward in +Y)
    # RS75 rebate depth = 3.5mm = 0.35cm

        ext_input.participantBodies = [box_body]
        feature = extrudes.add(ext_input)
        print(f"{'✓' if feature else '✗'} RS75 rebate ring cut {0.35}cm deep")

    for idx in screw_holes:
        profile = rebateSketch.profiles.item(idx)
        ext_input = extrudes.createInput(profile, adsk.fusion.FeatureOperations.CutFeatureOperation)
        ext_input.setDistanceExtent(False, ValueInput.createByReal(0.8))  # full baffle

        feature = extrudes.add(ext_input)
        print(f"{'✓' if feature else '✗'} Screw hole cut through {0.8}cm baffle")
    
    print(f"Script complete. Box should have rebates and screw holes now.")

# Check final box state
for body in root.bRepBodies:
    if body.name == "Box":
        print(f"Box faces: {body.faceCount}")

root = rootComp

# Create standoffs on the floor (Z = 0.5cm interior level)
# Amp mount pattern: 92mm x 64mm = 9.2cm x 6.4cm
# Standoff positions (from front face of box, interior):
# PCB center: X=6.5cm, Y=7.4cm from front face
# Pattern corners relative to center: ±4.6cm X, ±3.2cm Y
# Standoff hole radius: 0.21cm (M3 clearance)
# Standoff height: 0.6cm (6mm), creating a 6mm standoff above floor

# First, create the offset XY plane at Z = 0.5cm (interior floor)
planes = root.constructionPlanes
# Find the existing floor offset plane (Plane1)
floorPlane = None
for i in range(planes.count):
    p = planes.item(i)
    if p.name == "Plane1":
        floorPlane = p
        break

# Create a new offset plane at Z = 0.5cm if not found
if not floorPlane:
    planeInput = planes.createInput()
    planeInput.setByOffset(root.xYConstructionPlane, adsk.core.ValueInput.createByReal(0.5))
    floorPlane = planes.add(planeInput)

# Create sketch for standoff bases
sketches = root.sketches
standoffSketch = sketches.add(floorPlane)
standoffSketch.name = "StandoffBases_Sketch"
standoffSketch.isComputeDeferred = True

circles = standoffSketch.sketchCurves.sketchCircles

# Amp PCB mount pattern center: X=6.5cm, Y=7.4cm from front (world coords)
# But the offset plane at Z=0.5 maps to XY world coords
# Pattern: 9.2 x 6.4cm centered on (6.5, 7.4)
center_x = 6.5  # cm
center_y = 7.4  # cm
pat_half_w = 4.6  # cm
pat_half_d = 3.2  # cm

# 4 standoff positions at corners of 9.2 x 6.4cm pattern
positions = [
    (center_x - pat_half_w, center_y - pat_half_d),  # bottom-left
    (center_x + pat_half_w, center_y - pat_half_d),  # bottom-right
    (center_x - pat_half_w, center_y + pat_half_d),  # top-left
    (center_x + pat_half_w, center_y + pat_half_d),  # top-right
]

for (sx, sy) in positions:
    circles.addByCenterRadius(Point3D.create(sx, sy, 0), 0.4)  # 8mm diameter base

standoffSketch.isComputeDeferred = False

# Find the 4 circle profiles (each is a separate profile)
prof_count = standoffSketch.profiles.count
print(f"Standoff base profiles: {prof_count}")

# Extrude each as a JOIN (not new body) upward 0.6cm
box_body = None
for body in root.bRepBodies:
    if body.name == "Box":
        box_body = body
        break

standoff_bodies = []
extrudes = root.features.extrudeFeatures
for i in range(prof_count):
    profile = standoffSketch.profiles.item(i)
    area = profile.areaProperties().area
    # Each base circle area = π * 0.4² = 0.5027 cm²
    print(f"  Profile {i}: area = {area:.4f}")
    
    ext_input = extrudes.createInput(profile, adsk.fusion.FeatureOperations.JoinFeatureOperation)
    ext_input.setDistanceExtent(False, ValueInput.createByReal(0.6))  # 6mm height
    
    feature = extrudes.add(ext_input)
    if feature:
        for j in range(feature.bodies.count):
            b = feature.bodies.item(j)
            b.name = f"Standoff{j+1}"
            standoff_bodies.append(b)
            print(f"  Created standoff: {b.name}")

# Now create M3 insert holes on top of each standoff
# Need offset plane at Z = 1.1cm (0.5 + 0.6)
planes2 = root.constructionPlanes
planeInput2 = planes2.createInput()
planeInput2.setByOffset(root.xYConstructionPlane, adsk.core.ValueInput.createByReal(1.1))
standoffTopPlane = planes2.add(planeInput2)

standoffHoleSketch = sketches.add(standoffTopPlane)
standoffHoleSketch.name = "StandoffHoles_Sketch"

circles2 = standoffHoleSketch.sketchCurves.sketchCircles
for (sx, sy) in positions:
    circles2.addByCenterRadius(Point3D.create(sx, sy, 0), 0.21)  # M3 hole 4.2mm

standoffHoleProf_count = standoffHoleSketch.profiles.count
for i in range(standoffHoleProf_count):
    profile = standoffHoleSketch.profiles.item(i)
    ext_input = extrudes.createInput(profile, adsk.fusion.FeatureOperations.CutFeatureOperation)
    ext_input.setDistanceExtent(False, ValueInput.createByReal(-0.5))  # 5mm deep
    if standoff_bodies:
        # Cut through the first standoff
        ext_input.participantBodies = [standoff_bodies[0]]
    feature = extrudes.add(ext_input)
    if feature:
        print(f"  Standoff hole {i} cut through first standoff")

print(f"Script complete. {len(standoff_bodies)} standoffs created.")

# Check all bodies
for body in root.bRepBodies:
    print(f"  Body: {body.name}")

**Step 8:** Create external fillets (3mm) on vertical corners and top edges.

input.script:
root = rootComp

# Get the main body
main_body = root.bRepBodies.itemByName("Standoff1")
if not main_body:
    print("ERROR: Main body not found")
else:
    print(f"Main body: {main_body.name}")
    print(f"Total edges: {main_body.edges.count}")
    
    # We need to identify the vertical corners and top edges
    # Vertical corners: edges parallel to Z axis at the 4 bottom corners
    # Top edges: edges at Z=13cm (top face edges)
    
    # Let's find edges by position
    # The box is from X=0→20, Y=0→12, Z=0→13
    
    # Collect all edges, then identify which ones to fillet
    # Vertical edges at 4 corners (bottom):
    # - X=0, Y=0 (bottom-left front)
    # - X=20, Y=0 (bottom-right front)
    # - X=20, Y=12 (bottom-right back)
    # - X=0, Y=12 (bottom-left back)
    
    # Top horizontal edges (4 edges at Z=13):
    # - Along X at Y=0, Z=13
    # - Along X at Y=12, Z=13
    # - Along Y at X=0, Z=13
    # - Along Y at X=20, Z=13
    
    # We need to collect edges that are at the outer perimeter of the box
    # Let's find edges by examining their geometry
    
    vertical_edges = []
    top_edges = []
    
    for i in range(main_body.edges.count):
        edge = main_body.edges.item(i)
        try:
            # Get edge start and end points
            startPt = edge.geometry.startPoint
            endPt = edge.geometry.endPoint
            
            # Calculate midpoint
            midX = (startPt.x + endPt.x) / 2
            midY = (startPt.y + endPt.y) / 2
            midZ = (startPt.z + endPt.z) / 2
            
            # Check if this is a vertical edge (along Z axis)
            dz = abs(endPt.z - startPt.z)
            dx = abs(endPt.x - startPt.x)
            dy = abs(endPt.y - startPt.y)
            
            if dz > 0.1 and dx < 0.1 and dy < 0.1:
                # Vertical edge
                # Check if it's at a corner (at the outer perimeter)
                if (abs(midX) < 0.1 or abs(midX - 20) < 0.1):
                    if (abs(midY) < 0.1 or abs(midY - 12) < 0.1):
                        vertical_edges.append(i)
            elif dz < 0.1:
                # Horizontal edge
                if abs(midZ - 13) < 0.1:  # At top face
                    # Check if it's at the perimeter
                    if (abs(midX) < 0.1 or abs(midX - 20) < 0.1 or 
                        abs(midY) < 0.1 or abs(midY - 12) < 0.1):
                        top_edges.append(i)
        except:
            pass
    
    print(f"Vertical corners to fillet: {len(vertical_edges)} edges")
    print(f"Top edges to fillet: {len(top_edges)} edges")
    
    # Combine edges for fillet
    all_fillet_edges = vertical_edges + top_edges
    print(f"Total edges for 3mm fillet: {len(all_fillet_edges)}")
    
    # Create fillet using ObjectCollection
    fillets = root.features.filletFeatures
    filletInput = fillets.createInput()
    
    # Add edges
    edges = adsk.core.ObjectCollection.create()
    for i in all_fillet_edges:
        edges.add(main_body.edges.item(i))
    
    filletInput.addConstantRadiusEdgeSet(edges, ValueInput.createByReal(0.3), True)  # 3mm fillet
    
    feature = fillets.add(filletInput)
    if feature:
        print(f"Fillets applied successfully!")
    else:
        print("ERROR: Fillet feature failed")

root = rootComp

# Get bodies
main_body = root.bRepBodies.itemByName("Standoff1")
back_panel = root.bRepBodies.itemByName("Box")

print(f"Main body: {main_body.name}")
print(f"Back panel: {back_panel.name}")

# Create offset XZ plane at Y = 12cm (back face of main enclosure)
planes = root.constructionPlanes
planeInput = planes.createInput()
planeInput.setByOffset(root.xZConstructionPlane, adsk.core.ValueInput.createByReal(12.0))
backPlane = planes.add(planeInput)

# Create sketch for back panel holes
sketches = root.sketches
backHoleSketch = sketches.add(backPlane)
backHoleSketch.name = "BackPanelHoles_Sketch"
backHoleSketch.isComputeDeferred = True

circles = backHoleSketch.sketchCurves.sketchCircles

# Screw boss positions (X, Z from bottom-left of back panel)
# Bottom row: (1.2, 1.2), (10, 1.2), (18.8, 1.2)
# Top row: (1.2, 11.8), (10, 11.8), (18.8, 11.8)
boss_positions = [
    (1.2, 1.2), (10, 1.2), (18.8, 1.2),  # bottom
    (1.2, 11.8), (10, 11.8), (18.8, 11.8),  # top
]

for (bx, bz) in boss_positions:
    circles.addByCenterRadius(Point3D.create(bx, 0, bz), 0.16)  # 3.2mm diameter

# Power jack: X=17.5cm, Z=2.5cm, diameter 6.5mm
circles.addByCenterRadius(Point3D.create(17.5, 0, 2.5), 0.325)  # 6.5mm diameter

backHoleSketch.isComputeDeferred = False

# Verify profiles
prof_count = backHoleSketch.profiles.count
print(f"Back panel hole profiles: {prof_count}")
for i in range(prof_count):
    p = backHoleSketch.profiles.item(i)
    area = p.areaProperties().area
    print(f"  Profile {i}: area = {area:.4f}")

# Expected: 6 screw holes (area = π * 0.16² = 0.0804 each) + 1 jack (area = π * 0.325² = 0.3318)

# Extrude cuts through back panel thickness (0.5cm) in -Y direction (outward from box)
# Since the sketch is on the Y=12 plane and the back panel is at Y=12-12.5,
# we need to cut from Y=12 toward Y=12.5 (+Y direction)
# But wait - the back panel is at Y=12 to 12.5, and our sketch is at Y=12
# Cut in +Y direction (into the back panel body)

extrudes = root.features.extrudeFeatures
for i in range(prof_count):
    profile = backHoleSketch.profiles.item(i)
    area = profile.areaProperties().area
    ext_input = extrudes.createInput(profile, adsk.fusion.FeatureOperations.CutFeatureOperation)
    ext_input.setDistanceExtent(False, ValueInput.createByReal(0.5))  # full thickness
    
    # Cut through back panel only
    ext_input.participantBodies = [back_panel]
    
    feature = extrudes.add(ext_input)
    if feature:
        print(f"  Hole cut {i} succeeded")
    else:
        print(f"  Hole cut {i} FAILED")

# Verify back panel state
print(f"Back panel volume: {back_panel.areaProperties().volume:.4f}")
print(f"Back panel faces: {back_panel.faceCount}")

All 7 back panel holes succeeded. Now I'll create the port tube and the heat-set insert bosses.

**Step 11:** Create the port tube body.

input.script:
root = rootComp

# Create port tube: annular ring at port position (X=11.2, Z=2.3)
# Inner radius = 1.25cm, outer radius = 1.45cm
# Length = 8.3cm total (7.5cm inside + 0.8cm baffle)
# Tube is centered on the baffle face

# We need to create the tube along the Y axis (from front to inside the box)
# Sketch on XY plane at Z = 2.3cm (port height)

sketches = root.sketches
portSketch = sketches.add(root.xYConstructionPlane)
portSketch.name = "PortTube_Sketch"

circles = portSketch.sketchCurves.sketchCircles
circles.addByCenterRadius(Point3D.create(11.2, 0, 0), 1.45)  # outer radius
circles.addByCenterRadius(Point3D.create(11.2, 0, 0), 1.25)  # inner radius

# Find the ring profile (annulus between two circles)
prof_count = portSketch.profiles.count
print(f"Port tube profiles: {prof_count}")
for i in range(prof_count):
    p = portSketch.profiles.item(i)
    area = p.areaProperties().area
    # Ring area = π * (1.45² - 1.25²) = π * (2.1025 - 1.5625) = π * 0.54 = 1.6965 cm²
    print(f"  Profile {i}: area = {area:.4f}")

# Profile with area ~1.6965 is the ring
ring_profile = None
for i in range(prof_count):
    p = portSketch.profiles.item(i)
    area = p.areaProperties().area
    if 1.6 < area < 1.8:
        ring_profile = p
        print(f"  Found ring profile: {i}")
        break

if ring_profile:
    extrudes = root.features.extrudeFeatures
    ext_input = extrudes.createInput(ring_profile, adsk.fusion.FeatureOperations.NewBodyFeatureOperation)
    # Extrude along Y direction (into the box) by 8.3cm total length
    ext_input.setDistanceExtent(False, ValueInput.createByReal(8.3))
    
    feature = extrudes.add(ext_input)
    if feature:
        for b in feature.bodies:
            b.name = "Port_Tube"
            print(f"Created port tube: {b.name}")
    else:
        print("ERROR: Port tube extrude failed")
else:
    print("ERROR: No ring profile found")

# Check bodies
for body in root.bRepBodies:
    print(f"  Body: {body.name}, vol={body.areaProperties().volume:.4f}")

The enclosure looks solid with all cuts applied. Now I need to create the back panel mounting bosses on the inside of the enclosure.

**Step 12:** Create back panel mounting bosses (6 bosses for M3 heat-set inserts).

input.script:
root = rootComp

# Create boss circles on the inside back face of the enclosure (at Y=12, protruding inward into box)
# Boss positions (X, Z from bottom-left outer corner):
# Bottom row: (1.2, 1.2), (10, 1.2), (18.8, 1.2)
# Top row: (1.2, 11.8), (10, 11.8), (18.8, 11.8)
# Boss outer radius = 0.4cm (4mm), protrusion depth = 1.2cm into box

# Create offset XZ plane at Y = 12cm (inside face)
planes = root.constructionPlanes
planeInput = planes.createInput()
# Offset XZ plane to Y=12
planeInput.setByOffset(root.xZConstructionPlane, adsk.core.ValueInput.createByReal(12.0))
backFacePlane = planes.add(planeInput)

# Create sketch for boss positions
sketches = root.sketches
bossSketch = sketches.add(backFacePlane)
bossSketch.name = "BackBosses_Sketch"
bossSketch.isComputeDeferred = True

circles = bossSketch.sketchCurves.sketchCircles

# Boss positions (X in cm on XZ plane maps to world X, Y maps to world -Z but on Y=12 plane
# Actually, boss positions are given in X, Z coords on the back panel face
# On XZ plane at Y=12, sketch coords: X = world X, Y = world Z (since it's on the back face)
# Boss X positions: 1.2, 10, 18.8 cm
# Boss Z positions: 1.2, 11.8 cm

boss_positions = [
    (1.2, 1.2), (10.0, 1.2), (18.8, 1.2),  # bottom row
    (1.2, 11.8), (10.0, 11.8), (18.8, 11.8),  # top row
]

for (bx, bz) in boss_positions:
    circles.addByCenterRadius(Point3D.create(bx, 0, bz), 0.4)  # 8mm diameter boss base

bossSketch.isComputeDeferred = False

# Verify profiles
prof_count = bossSketch.profiles.count
print(f"Boss profiles: {prof_count}")

# Find the 6 boss profiles (each should be π*0.4² = 0.5027 cm²)
boss_profiles = []
for i in range(prof_count):
    p = bossSketch.profiles.item(i)
    area = p.areaProperties().area
    print(f"  Profile {i}: area = {area:.4f}")
    if 0.45 < area < 0.56:
        boss_profiles.append(i)

print(f"Found {len(boss_profiles)} boss profiles")

# Extrude bosses inward (in +Y direction, into the box) by 1.2cm
# From Y=12 plane, extrude +Y (into the enclosure interior)
extrudes = root.features.extrudeFeatures
main_body = root.bRepBodies.itemByName("Standoff1")

for idx in boss_profiles:
    profile = bossSketch.profiles.item(idx)
    ext_input = extrudes.createInput(profile, adsk.fusion.FeatureOperations.JoinFeatureOperation)
    ext_input.setDistanceExtent(False, ValueInput.createByReal(1.2))  # 12mm boss height
    
    # Join to main body
    ext_input.participantBodies = [main_body]
    
    feature = extrudes.add(ext_input)
    if feature:
        print(f"  Boss {idx} joined successfully")
    else:
        print(f"  Boss {idx} extrude FAILED")

# Now create the boss hole (insert hole) on the back face
# These holes go through the bosses from the OUTSIDE (back panel side)
# Create a sketch on the back face (Y=12.5) with 4.2mm holes

planes2 = root.constructionPlanes
planeInput2 = planes2.createInput()
planeInput2.setByOffset(root.xZConstructionPlane, adsk.core.ValueInput.createByReal(12.5))
backOuterPlane = planes2.add(planeInput2)

bossHoleSketch = sketches.add(backOuterPlane)
bossHoleSketch.name = "BossInsertHoles_Sketch"

circles2 = bossHoleSketch.sketchCurves.sketchCircles
for (bx, bz) in boss_positions:
    circles2.addByCenterRadius(Point3D.create(bx, 0, bz), 0.21)  # 4.2mm M3 insert hole

bossHoleProfCount = bossHoleSketch.profiles.count
for i in range(bossHoleProfCount):
    profile = bossHoleSketch.profiles.item(i)
    ext_input = extrudes.createInput(profile, adsk.fusion.FeatureOperations.CutFeatureOperation)
    # Cut from back face inward through the boss (1.2cm)
    ext_input.setDistanceExtent(False, ValueInput.createByReal(1.2))
    
    # Cut through the boss bodies (which are joined to main body)
    ext_input.participantBodies = [main_body]
    
    feature = extrudes.add(ext_input)
    if feature:
        print(f"  Insert hole {i} cut successfully")
    else:
        print(f"  Insert hole {i} FAILED")

print(f"Boss creation complete")
print(f"Total bodies: {len([b for b in root.bRepBodies])}")

content.text: I notice the back panel bosses were created with incorrect Z positions due to XZ plane coordinate mapping issues. Let me fix this with a targeted script.

input.script:
root = rootComp

# First, let's delete the incorrectly positioned boss bodies and back panel
bodies_to_delete = ["Body7","Body8","Body9","Body10","Body11","Body12","Box"]
for name in bodies_to_delete:
    body = root.bRepBodies.itemByName(name)
    if body:
        body.deleteMe()
        print(f"Deleted {name}")
    else:
        print(f"Not found: {name}")

# Now recreate back panel at correct position (Y=12 to 12.5)
sketches = root.sketches
backPanelSketch = sketches.add(root.xYConstructionPlane)
backPanelSketch.name = "BackPanel_Sketch"
lines = backPanelSketch.sketchCurves.sketchLines
# Rectangle from X=0,Y=12 to X=20,Y=12.5 on XY plane
# Since we're on XY plane, Z=0 is the plane
lines.addTwoPointRectangle(
    Point3D.create(0, 12.0, 0),
    Point3D.create(20.0, 12.5, 0)
)
# Find the rectangle profile
prof_count = backPanelSketch.profiles.count
print(f"Back panel profiles: {prof_count}")
for i in range(prof_count):
    p = backPanelSketch.profiles.item(i)
    area = p.areaProperties().area
    print(f"  Profile {i}: area = {area:.4f}")

# Extrude to create back panel body
extrudes = root.features.extrudeFeatures
if prof_count > 0:
    ext_input = extrudes.createInput(backPanelSketch.profiles.item(0), adsk.fusion.FeatureOperations.NewBodyFeatureOperation)
    # Extrude along Z by 13cm (to create a full panel)
    ext_input.setDistanceExtent(False, ValueInput.createByReal(13.0))
    feature = extrudes.add(ext_input)
    if feature:
        for b in feature.bodies:
            b.name = "Back_Panel"
            print(f"Created back panel: {b.name}")
    else:
        print("ERROR: Back panel extrude failed")
else:
    print("ERROR: No back panel profile found")

# Now create back panel holes at CORRECT positions
# Use offset XZ plane at Y=12.5 for hole positions
planes = root.constructionPlanes
planeInput = planes.createInput()
planeInput.setByOffset(root.xZConstructionPlane, adsk.core.ValueInput.createByReal(12.5))
backHolePlane = planes.add(planeInput)

# Create sketch for back panel holes
backHoleSketch = sketches.add(backHolePlane)
backHoleSketch.name = "BackPanelHoles_Final_Sketch"
backHoleSketch.isComputeDeferred = True

circles = backHoleSketch.sketchCurves.sketchCircles

# Boss positions (X, Z) on the back face:
# Bottom row: (1.2, 1.2), (10, 1.2), (18.8, 1.2)
# Top row: (1.2, 11.8), (10, 11.8), (18.8, 11.8)
# On XZ plane: point.x=X, point.y=-Z (negated mapping)
# So: Point3D.create(X, -Z, 0)

boss_positions = [
    (1.2, -1.2), (10.0, -1.2), (18.8, -1.2),  # bottom row (X, -Z)
    (1.2, -11.8), (10.0, -11.8), (18.8, -11.8),  # top row
]

for (bx, bz) in boss_positions:
    circles.addByCenterRadius(Point3D.create(bx, bz, 0), 0.16)  # 3.2mm diameter (M3 clearance)

# Power jack hole
circles.addByCenterRadius(Point3D.create(17.5, -2.5, 0), 0.325)  # 6.5mm diameter

backHoleSketch.isComputeDeferred = False

# Verify profiles
hprof_count = backHoleSketch.profiles.count
print(f"Back panel hole profiles: {hprof_count}")

# Find screw hole profiles (area = π * 0.16² = 0.0804) and jack profile (area = π * 0.325² = 0.3318)
screw_holes = []
jack_hole = None
for i in range(hprof_count):
    p = backHoleSketch.profiles.item(i)
    area = p.areaProperties().area
    print(f"  Profile {i}: area = {area:.4f}")
    if 0.07 < area < 0.09:
        screw_holes.append(i)
    elif 0.3 < area < 0.36:
        jack_hole = i

# Get the back panel body
back_panel = root.bRepBodies.itemByName("Back_Panel")
if not back_panel:
    print("ERROR: Back panel not found")
else:
    # Cut screw holes
    for idx in screw_holes:
        profile = backHoleSketch.profiles.item(idx)
        ext_input = extrudes.createInput(profile, adsk.fusion.FeatureOperations.CutFeatureOperation)
        ext_input.setDistanceExtent(False, ValueInput.createByReal(0.5))  # 5mm thickness
        ext_input.participantBodies = [back_panel]
        feature = extrudes.add(ext_input)
        if feature:
            print(f"  Screw hole {idx} cut successfully")

    # Cut power jack
    if jack_hole is not None:
        profile = backHoleSketch.profiles.item(jack_hole)
        ext_input = extrudes.createInput(profile, adsk.fusion.FeatureOperations.CutFeatureOperation)
        ext_input.setDistanceExtent(False, ValueInput.createByReal(0.5))
        ext_input.participantBodies = [back_panel]
        feature = extrudes.add(ext_input)
        if feature:
            print(f"  Power jack cut successfully")

print(f"Back panel and holes recreated")

# Now create bosses at correct positions
# Offset XZ plane at Y=12 (inside back face)
planeInput2 = planes.createInput()
planeInput2.setByOffset(root.xZConstructionPlane, adsk.core.ValueInput.createByReal(12.0))
bossPlane = planes.add(planeInput2)

bossSketch = sketches.add(bossPlane)
bossSketch.name = "Bosses_Final_Sketch"
bossSketch.isComputeDeferred = True

circles2 = bossSketch.sketchCurves.sketchCircles
# Boss positions: X, -Z (negated Z mapping on XZ plane)
# Boss Z positions: 1.2 and 11.8 → sketch Y = -1.2 and -11.8
boss_positions2 = [
    (1.2, -1.2), (10.0, -1.2), (18.8, -1.2),  # bottom row
    (1.2, -11.8), (10.0, -11.8), (18.8, -11.8),  # top row
]

for (bx, bz) in boss_positions2:
    circles2.addByCenterRadius(Point3D.create(bx, bz, 0), 0.4)  # 8mm diameter boss base

bossSketch.isComputeDeferred = False

# Find boss profiles
bp_prof_count = bossSketch.profiles.count
boss_profiles = []
for i in range(bp_prof_count):
    p = bossSketch.profiles.item(i)
    area = p.areaProperties().area
    print(f"Boss profile {i}: area = {area:.4f}")
    if 0.45 < area < 0.56:
        boss_profiles.append(i)

# Get main body
main_body = root.bRepBodies.itemByName("Standoff1")

# Extrude bosses inward (+Y direction, into the box) by 1.2cm
for idx in boss_profiles:
    profile = bossSketch.profiles.item(idx)
    ext_input = extrudes.createInput(profile, adsk.fusion.FeatureOperations.JoinFeatureOperation)
    ext_input.setDistanceExtent(False, ValueInput.createByReal(1.2))  # 12mm boss height
    ext_input.participantBodies = [main_body]
    feature = extrudes.add(ext_input)
    if feature:
        print(f"  Boss {idx} joined successfully")

# Now create insert holes in bosses (4.2mm dia, from back face)
# Use offset XZ plane at Y=12.5
planeInput3 = planes.createInput()
planeInput3.setByOffset(root.xZConstructionPlane, adsk.core.ValueInput.createByReal(12.5))
insertPlane = planes.add(planeInput3)

insertHoleSketch = sketches.add(insertPlane)
insertHoleSketch.name = "InsertHoles_Sketch"

circles3 = insertHoleSketch.sketchCurves.sketchCircles
for (bx, bz) in boss_positions2:
    circles3.addByCenterRadius(Point3D.create(bx, bz, 0), 0.21)  # 4.2mm M3 insert hole

ih_prof_count = insertHoleSketch.profiles.count
for i in range(ih_prof_count):
    profile = insertHoleSketch.profiles.item(i)
    ext_input = extrudes.createInput(profile, adsk.fusion.FeatureOperations.CutFeatureOperation)
    ext_input.setDistanceExtent(False, ValueInput.createByReal(1.2))  # through boss depth
    ext_input.participantBodies = [main_body]
    feature = extrudes.add(ext_input)
    if feature:
        print(f"  Insert hole {i} cut successfully")

print(f"All bodies recreated correctly")
print(f"Total bodies: {root.bRepBodies.count}")
for body in root.bRepBodies:
    print(f"  {body.name}")

2. **Back panel positioning** — Wrong Y placement twice, required deletion and recreation
3. **Port tube** — Placed at wrong coordinates, had to delete and remake
4. **Volume mismatch** — Enclosure shows 709 cm³ but internal volume should be ~2.5L (2500 cm³). The math doesn't check out.
5. **Save failures** — Couldn't save the document despite trying multiple times

**What's actually working:**
- ✅ Base enclosure box (200×120×130mm)
- ✅ Interior hollowing

- ✅ Driver flange rebates

- ✅ Center anti-resonance rib
- ✅ External 3mm fillets
- ✅ 4 amp standoffs on floor
- ✅ Port tube body
- ✅ Back panel body (just recreated correctly)
- ✅ 6 bosses + insert holes on main enclosure (just recreated)

**The fix:**
I need to start over with the XZ plane coordinate mapping firmly in mind. Every sketch on XZ must use `Point3D.create(x, -z, 0)` for world Z coordinates. content.text: Good - the body list shows Standoff1 (main enclosure with bosses), Port_Tube, and Back_Panel. The Y=13.2 max confirms bosses extend outward 1.2cm beyond the box. I need to cut them out and remake them pointing inward.

input.script:
root = rootComp

main_body = root.bRepBodies.itemByName("Standoff1")
boss_positions = [
    (1.2, -1.2), (10.0, -1.2), (18.8, -1.2),  # bottom row (X, -Z on XZ plane)
    (1.2, -11.8), (10.0, -11.8), (18.8, -11.8),  # top row
]

# Step 1: Cut out the outward-extending bosses
# They are at Y=12 to Y=13.2, so cut from Y=12.5 (back face) inward to Y=12
# Use offset XZ plane at Y=12.5
planes = root.constructionPlanes
planeInput = planes.createInput()
planeInput.setByOffset(root.xZConstructionPlane, adsk.core.ValueInput.createByReal(12.5))
cutPlane = planes.add(planeInput)

sketches = root.sketches
bossCutSketch = sketches.add(cutPlane)
bossCutSketch.name = "BossCut_Sketch"
bossCutSketch.isComputeDeferred = True

circles = bossCutSketch.sketchCurves.sketchCircles
for (bx, bz) in boss_positions:
    circles.addByCenterRadius(Point3D.create(bx, bz, 0), 0.4)  # 8mm radius to match boss

bossCutSketch.isComputeDeferred = False

# Cut these bosses out by cutting from the back face into the box
# The bosses extend from Y=12 to Y=13.2 (outside the box)
# We need to cut them in the -Y direction (from Y=12.5 back to Y=12)
extrudes = root.features.extrudeFeatures

# We need to cut in -Y direction (into the box, removing the outward bosses)
# But the bosses are at the back face (Y=12) extending to Y=13.2
# Cutting from Y=12.5 toward -Y by 1.2cm will remove the bosses

for i in range(bossCutSketch.profiles.count):
    profile = bossCutSketch.profiles.item(i)
    area = profile.areaProperties().area
    ext_input = extrudes.createInput(profile, adsk.fusion.FeatureOperations.CutFeatureOperation)
    # Cut 1.2cm in negative Y direction (removes the outward bosses)
    ext_input.setDistanceExtent(False, ValueInput.createByReal(-1.2))
    ext_input.participantBodies = [main_body]
    
    feature = extrudes.add(ext_input)
    if feature:
        print(f"  Boss cut {i} succeeded (area={area:.4f})")
    else:
        print(f"  Boss cut {i} FAILED")

print(f"After boss cut, bodies: {root.bRepBodies.count}")

input.script:
root = rootComp

main_body = root.bRepBodies.itemByName("Standoff1")
boss_positions = [
    (1.2, -1.2), (10.0, -1.2), (18.8, -1.2),
    (1.2, -11.8), (10.0, -11.8), (18.8, -11.8),
]

# Create boss sketch on XZ plane at Y=12 (inside back face)
# These will extrude INWARD (-Y direction) by 1.2cm
planes = root.constructionPlanes
planeInput = planes.createInput()
planeInput.setByOffset(root.xZConstructionPlane, adsk.core.ValueInput.createByReal(12.0))
bossPlane = planes.add(planeInput)

sketches = root.sketches
newBossSketch = sketches.add(bossPlane)
newBossSketch.name = "InnerBosses_Sketch"
newBossSketch.isComputeDeferred = True

circles = newBossSketch.sketchCurves.sketchCircles
for (bx, bz) in boss_positions:
    circles.addByCenterRadius(Point3D.create(bx, bz, 0), 0.4)

newBossSketch.isComputeDeferred = False

# Extrude bosses in -Y direction (into the enclosure interior) by 1.2cm
extrudes = root.features.extrudeFeatures

for i in range(newBossSketch.profiles.count):
    profile = newBossSketch.profiles.item(i)
    area = profile.areaProperties().area
    ext_input = extrudes.createInput(profile, adsk.fusion.FeatureOperations.JoinFeatureOperation)
    ext_input.setDistanceExtent(False, ValueInput.createByReal(-1.2))  # -Y = inward
    ext_input.participantBodies = [main_body]
    
    feature = extrudes.add(ext_input)
    if feature:
        print(f"  Inner boss {i} joined (area={area:.4f})")
    else:
        print(f"  Inner boss {i} FAILED")

print(f"Inner bosses created")

# Now cut the insert holes from the back face (Y=12.5)
# These go into the main body by 1.2cm (through the boss depth)
planeInput2 = planes.createInput()
planeInput2.setByOffset(root.xZConstructionPlane, adsk.core.ValueInput.createByReal(12.5))
insertPlane = planes.add(planeInput2)

insertHoleSketch = sketches.add(insertPlane)
insertHoleSketch.name = "InsertHoles_Final_Sketch"

circles2 = insertHoleSketch.sketchCurves.sketchCircles
for (bx, bz) in boss_positions:
    circles2.addByCenterRadius(Point3D.create(bx, bz, 0), 0.21)  # 4.2mm

ih_prof_count = insertHoleSketch.profiles.count
for i in range(ih_prof_count):
    profile = insertHoleSketch.profiles.item(i)
    ext_input = extrudes.createInput(profile, adsk.fusion.FeatureOperations.CutFeatureOperation)
    # Cut 1.2cm into the back face (through the boss)
    ext_input.setDistanceExtent(False, ValueInput.createByReal(-1.2))
    ext_input.participantBodies = [main_body]
    
    feature = extrudes.add(ext_input)
    if feature:
        print(f"  Insert hole {i} cut")
    else:
        print(f"  Insert hole {i} FAILED")

# Check final state
for body in root.bRepBodies:
    print(f"  {body.name}: vol={body.volume_properties().volume:.4f}" if hasattr(body, 'volume_properties') else f"  {body.name}")

input.script:
root = rootComp

# The main body
body = root.bRepBodies.itemByName("Standoff1")

# Check all faces to find the front face (at Y=0)
front_face = None
for i in range(body.faces.count):
    face = body.faces.item(i)
    # Check if this is a planar face at Y=0
    geo = face.geometry
    if hasattr(geo, 'normal'):
        normal = geo.normal
        # A face at Y=0 would have normal along Y axis
        # For front face, normal should point in -Y direction (facing out)
        if abs(normal.y + 1) < 0.1:  # facing toward -Y
            bb = face.boundingBox
            print(f"Face {i}: planar at Y=0, bbox min={bb.minPoint}, max={bb.maxPoint}, area={face.area}")
            front_face = face
        elif abs(normal.y - 1) < 0.1:  # facing +Y (back face)
            bb = face.boundingBox
            print(f"Face {i}: planar at Y=12, bbox min={bb.minPoint}, max={bb.maxPoint}, area={face.area}")

if front_face:
    print(f"Found front baffle face: index {front_face}")
    
    # Check vertices of the front face to see what cutouts exist
    print(f"Front face edges: {len(list(front_face.edges))}")
    
    # Check edge centers to find circular holes
    for i, edge in enumerate(front_face.edges):
        if hasattr(edge, 'geometry') and hasattr(edge.geometry, 'radius'):
            center = edge.geometry.center
            print(f"  Circular edge {i}: center=({center.x:.3f}, {center.y:.3f}, {center.z:.3f}), radius={edge.geometry.radius:.3f}")
else:
    print("No front face at Y=0 found")

# Also check the side faces to see if cutouts ended up there
for i in range(body.faces.count):
    face = body.faces.item(i)
    geo = face.geometry
    if hasattr(geo, 'normal'):
        normal = geo.normal
        if abs(normal.x) < 0.1:  # X-facing face
            bb = face.boundingBox
            print(f"X-facing face {i}: min={bb.minPoint}, max={bb.maxPoint}")
        elif abs(normal.z) < 0.1:  # Z-facing face
            bb = face.boundingBox
            print(f"Z-facing face {i}: min={bb.minPoint}, max={bb.maxPoint}")

input.script:
root = rootComp

# Get the main body
body = root.bRepBodies.itemByName("Standoff1")

# Find the front face at Y=0 (the baffle)
# Look for faces whose centroid is near Y=0
front_faces = []
for i in range(body.faces.count):
    face = body.faces.item(i)
    geo = face.geometry
    if hasattr(geo, 'surfaceType') and geo.surfaceType == 1:  # Planar surface
        centroid = face.area.centroid
        if abs(centroid.y) < 0.1:  # At Y=0
            area = face.area.area
            bb = face.boundingBox
            front_faces.append((i, area, centroid, bb))
            print(f"Face {i} at Y=0: area={area:.4f}, centroid=({centroid.x:.3f}, {centroid.y:.3f}, {centroid.z:.3f})")
            print(f"  bbox: min=({bb.minPoint.x:.3f}, {bb.minPoint.y:.3f}, {bb.minPoint.z:.3f}), max=({bb.maxPoint.x:.3f}, {bb.maxPoint.y:.3f}, {bb.maxPoint.z:.3f})")

# Now find ALL circular faces on the entire body (these would be cutout holes)
circular_faces = []
for i in range(body.faces.count):
    face = body.faces.item(i)
    geo = face.geometry
    if hasattr(geo, 'surfaceType'):
        st = geo.surfaceType
        if st == 3:  # Cylinder surface type
            radius = geo.radius
            center = geo.origin
            circular_faces.append((i, radius, center))
            print(f"Cylindrical face {i}: radius={radius:.4f}, center=({center.x:.3f}, {center.y:.3f}, {center.z:.3f})")

root = rootComp

# 1. Create offset XY plane at Z = 0.5cm (interior floor)
planes = root.constructionPlanes
planeInput = planes.createInput()
planeInput.setByOffset(root.xYConstructionPlane, adsk.core.ValueInput.createByReal(0.5))
floorPlane = planes.add(planeInput)

# 2. Sketch interior cut rectangle on this plane
sketches = root.sketches
interiorSketch = sketches.add(floorPlane)
interiorSketch.name = "InteriorCut_Sketch"
interiorSketch.isComputeDeferred = True

# Interior dimensions: 5mm walls on all sides
# X: 0.5 → 19.5 (5mm each side)
# Y: 0.5 → 11.5 (5mm back, 0.5mm front wall for floor)
# This rectangle on XY plane maps directly to world XY
lines = interiorSketch.sketchCurves.sketchLines
lines.addTwoPointRectangle(
    Point3D.create(0.5, 0.5, 0),
    Point3D.create(19.5, 11.5, 0)
)

interiorSketch.isComputeDeferred = False

# Verify profile
prof_count = interiorSketch.profiles.count
print(f"Interior profiles: {prof_count}")
for i in range(prof_count):
    p = interiorSketch.profiles.item(i)
    area = p.areaProperties().area
    print(f"  Profile {i}: area = {area:.4f}")

# Expected: 19 * 11 = 209 cm²
expected = 19.0 * 11.0
best_idx = 0
best_diff = float('inf')
for i in range(prof_count):
    p = interiorSketch.profiles.item(i)
    area = p.areaProperties().area
    diff = abs(area - expected)
    if diff < best_diff:
        best_diff = diff
        best_idx = i

# 3. Extrude cut UPWARD (+Z) by 12cm
box_body = root.bRepBodies.itemByName("Box")
extrudes = root.features.extrudeFeatures
ext_input = extrudes.createInput(interiorSketch.profiles.item(best_idx), adsk.fusion.FeatureOperations.CutFeatureOperation)
ext_input.setDistanceExtent(False, ValueInput.createByReal(12.0))
ext_input.participantBodies = [box_body]

feature = extrudes.add(ext_input)
if feature:
    print("Interior cut succeeded")
else:
    print("ERROR: Interior cut FAILED")

content.text:
Volume correct (612 cm³). Now the critical step: **baffle cutouts on XZ plane with correct coordinate mapping.**

**Critical rule:** On XZ plane, `Point3D.create(x, sketchY, 0)` maps to world `(x, 0, -sketchY)`. So to place at world Z=7.0cm, I use `sketchY = -7.0`.

input.script:
root = rootComp

# Create offset XZ plane at Y = -0.01cm (just outside front face)
planes = root.constructionPlanes
planeInput = planes.createInput()
planeInput.setByOffset(root.xZConstructionPlane, adsk.core.ValueInput.createByReal(-0.01))
bafflePlane = planes.add(planeInput)

# Sketch driver cutouts, port hole, control holes on this plane
# CRITICAL: on XZ plane, sketch Y maps to world -Z
# So world Z = -sketchY
sketches = root.sketches
baffleSketch = sketches.add(bafflePlane)
baffleSketch.name = "BaffleHoles_Sketch"
baffleSketch.isComputeDeferred = True

circles = baffleSketch.sketchCurves.sketchCircles

# Driver cutouts (radius in cm)
# W3-2141: center at world (X=7, Z=7) → sketch (7, -7)
circles.addByCenterRadius(Point3D.create(7.0, -7.0, 0), 3.71)  # W3 cutout Ø74.2mm
# RS75-4: center at world (X=15.3, Z=7) → sketch (15.3, -7)
circles.addByCenterRadius(Point3D.create(15.3, -7.0, 0), 2.705)  # RS75 cutout Ø54.1mm

# Port: center at world (X=11.2, Z=2.3) → sketch (11.2, -2.3)
circles.addByCenterRadius(Point3D.create(11.2, -2.3, 0), 1.25)  # Port Ø25mm

# POT holes: center Z=2.2 → sketchY = -2.2
circles.addByCenterRadius(Point3D.create(14.2, -2.2, 0), 0.35)  # POT1
circles.addByCenterRadius(Point3D.create(16.0, -2.2, 0), 0.35)  # POT2
circles.addByCenterRadius(Point3D.create(17.8, -2.2, 0), 0.35)  # POT3

# LED hole: center Z=2.2 → sketchY = -2.2
circles.addByCenterRadius(Point3D.create(12.7, -2.2, 0), 0.25)  # LED Ø5mm

baffleSketch.isComputeDeferred = False

# Verify profiles
prof_count = baffleSketch.profiles.count
print(f"Baffle hole profiles: {prof_count}")
for i in range(prof_count):
    p = baffleSketch.profiles.item(i)
    area = p.areaProperties().area
    print(f"  Profile {i}: area = {area:.4f}")

# Extrude each cut INWARD (+Y direction, into the box) through baffle thickness 0.8cm
box_body = root.bRepBodies.itemByName("Box")
extrudes = root.features.extrudeFeatures

for i in range(prof_count):
    profile = baffleSketch.profiles.item(i)
    ext_input = extrudes.createInput(profile, adsk.fusion.FeatureOperations.CutFeatureOperation)
    ext_input.setDistanceExtent(False, ValueInput.createByReal(0.8))  # 8mm baffle
    ext_input.participantBodies = [box_body]
    
    feature = extrudes.add(ext_input)
    if feature:
        print(f"  Cut {i} succeeded")
    else:
        print(f"  Cut {i} FAILED")

print(f"Done. Box volume should be reduced.")

# Now create flange rebates (annular counterbores on front face)
# These need to be cut from the front face going inward +Y
# Offset XZ plane at Y = -0.02cm
planes2 = root.constructionPlanes
planeInput2 = planes2.createInput()
planeInput2.setByOffset(root.xZConstructionPlane, adsk.core.ValueInput.createByReal(-0.02))
rebatePlane = planes2.add(planeInput2)

rebateSketch = sketches.add(rebatePlane)
rebateSketch.name = "FlangeRebates_Sketch"
rebateSketch.isComputeDeferred = True

circles2 = rebateSketch.sketchCurves.sketchCircles

# W3 flange rebate: outer ring at r=4.605cm, cutout at r=3.71cm
# Since cutout circle was already cut, this creates an outer ring
circles2.addByCenterRadius(Point3D.create(7.0, -7.0, 0), 4.605)  # W3 flange OD
# RS75 flange rebate: outer ring at r=3.59cm
circles2.addByCenterRadius(Point3D.create(15.3, -7.0, 0), 3.59)  # RS75 flange OD

# W3 mounting holes: bolt circle r=4.286cm at 45/135/225/315 degrees
bolt_r = 4.286
for deg in [45, 135, 225, 315]:
    rad = math.radians(deg)
    hx = 7.0 + bolt_r * math.cos(rad)
    hz = 7.0 + bolt_r * math.sin(rad)
    circles2.addByCenterRadius(Point3D.create(hx, -hz, 0), 0.225)  # 4.5mm diameter

# RS75 mounting holes: bolt circle r=3.2cm at 45/135/225/315 degrees
bolt_r2 = 3.2
for deg in [45, 135, 225, 315]:
    rad = math.radians(deg)
    hx = 15.3 + bolt_r2 * math.cos(rad)
    hz = 7.0 + bolt_r2 * math.sin(rad)
    circles2.addByCenterRadius(Point3D.create(hx, -hz, 0), 0.21)  # 4.2mm diameter

rebateSketch.isComputeDeferred = False

prof2_count = rebateSketch.profiles.count
print(f"\nRebate profiles: {prof2_count}")
for i in range(prof2_count):
    p = rebateSketch.profiles.item(i)
    area = p.areaProperties().area
    print(f"  Profile {i}: area = {area:.4f}")

# Rebate rings: W3 area = π*(4.605²-3.71²) = 24.4 cm², RS75 area = π*(3.59²-2.705²) = 18.1 cm²
# Screw holes: small areas ~0.16 cm²

for i in range(prof2_count):
    p = rebateSketch.profiles.item(i)
    area = p.areaProperties().area
    
    # Rebate rings go 4mm deep (W3) or 3.5mm (RS75) into the face
    # Screw holes go full 8mm through
    if 23 < area < 26:  # W3 rebate ring
        depth = 0.4  # 4mm
    elif 17 < area < 20:  # RS75 rebate ring
        depth = 0.35  # 3.5mm
    else:  # Screw holes (full baffle depth)
        depth = 0.8
    
    ext_input = extrudes.createInput(p, adsk.fusion.FeatureOperations.CutFeatureOperation)
    ext_input.setDistanceExtent(False, ValueInput.createByReal(depth))
    ext_input.participantBodies = [box_body]
    
    feature = extrudes.add(ext_input)
    if feature:
        print(f"  Rebate/screw cut {i} (area={area:.4f}, depth={depth}) succeeded")
    else:
        print(f"  Rebate/screw cut {i} FAILED")

print(f"Script complete")

# Check final volume
for body in root.bRepBodies:
    print(f"  {body.name}: vol={body.volume_properties().volume:.4f}")

import math

root = rootComp
sketches = root.sketches
planes = root.constructionPlanes
extrudes = root.features.extrudeFeatures

# ============================================================
# STEP 1: Create base solid box (200x120x130mm)
# ============================================================
boxSketch = sketches.add(root.xYConstructionPlane)
boxSketch.name = "BoxBase_Sketch"
lines = boxSketch.sketchCurves.sketchLines
lines.addTwoPointRectangle(Point3D.create(0, 0, 0), Point3D.create(20, 12, 0))
box_prof = boxSketch.profiles.item(0)
ext = extrudes.createInput(box_prof, adsk.fusion.FeatureOperations.NewBodyFeatureOperation)
ext.setDistanceExtent(False, ValueInput.createByReal(13.0))
boxFeat = extrudes.add(ext)
boxBody = None
for b in boxFeat.bodies:
    b.name = "Enclosure_Shell"
    boxBody = b
print(f"1. Box created: {boxBody.name}")

# ============================================================
# STEP 2: Hollow the interior (5mm walls on all sides)
# ============================================================
# Offset XY plane at Z = 0.5cm
pIn = planes.createInput()
pIn.setByOffset(root.xYConstructionPlane, ValueInput.createByReal(0.5))
floorPlane = planes.add(pIn)

intSketch = sketches.add(floorPlane)
intSketch.name = "InteriorCut"
intSketch.isComputeDeferred = True
iLines = intSketch.sketchCurves.sketchLines
# Interior: X=0.5→19.5 (5mm side walls), Y=0.8→11.5 (8mm front wall, 5mm back wall)
iLines.addTwoPointRectangle(Point3D.create(0.5, 0.8, 0), Point3D.create(19.5, 11.5, 0))
intSketch.isComputeDeferred = False
int_prof = intSketch.profiles.item(0)
ext2 = extrudes.createInput(int_prof, adsk.fusion.FeatureOperations.CutFeatureOperation)
ext2.setDistanceExtent(False, ValueInput.createByReal(12.0))
ext2.participantBodies = [boxBody]
extrudes.add(ext2)
print(f"2. Interior hollowed")

# ============================================================
# STEP 3: Front baffle cutouts (driver, port, POT, LED)
# ============================================================
# CRITICAL: XZ plane mapping: Point3D.create(x, -z_world, 0) → world (x, 0, z_world)
# Offset XZ plane at Y = -0.01 (just outside front face)
pB = planes.createInput()
pB.setByOffset(root.xZConstructionPlane, ValueInput.createByReal(-0.01))
bafflePlane = planes.add(pB)

baffleSketch = sketches.add(bafflePlane)
baffleSketch.name = "BaffleCuts"
baffleSketch.isComputeDeferred = True
bCircles = baffleSketch.sketchCurves.sketchCircles

# Driver cutouts: r in cm
bCircles.addByCenterRadius(Point3D.create(7.0, -7.0, 0), 3.71)    # W3-2141 Ø74.2mm
bCircles.addByCenterRadius(Point3D.create(15.3, -7.0, 0), 2.705)   # RS75-4 Ø54.1mm
bCircles.addByCenterRadius(Point3D.create(11.2, -2.3, 0), 1.25)    # Port Ø25mm
bCircles.addByCenterRadius(Point3D.create(14.2, -2.2, 0), 0.35)    # POT1 Ø7mm
bCircles.addByCenterRadius(Point3D.create(16.0, -2.2, 0), 0.35)    # POT2 Ø7mm
bCircles.addByCenterRadius(Point3D.create(17.8, -2.2, 0), 0.35)    # POT3 Ø7mm
bCircles.addByCenterRadius(Point3D.create(12.7, -2.2, 0), 0.25)    # LED Ø5mm
baffleSketch.isComputeDeferred = False

bc_prof_count = baffleSketch.profiles.count
for i in range(bc_prof_count):
    prof = baffleSketch.profiles.item(i)
    ext3 = extrudes.createInput(prof, adsk.fusion.FeatureOperations.CutFeatureOperation)
    ext3.setDistanceExtent(False, ValueInput.createByReal(0.8))  # 8mm baffle thickness
    ext3.participantBodies = [boxBody]
    feat = extrudes.add(ext3)
    if not feat:
        print(f"  WARNING: Baffle cut {i} failed")

# Verify baffle face has circular holes
face_at_y0 = None
for i in range(boxBody.faces.count):
    face = boxBody.faces.item(i)
    geo = face.geometry
    if hasattr(geo, 'surfaceType') and str(geo.surfaceType) == 'adsk::core::SurfaceTypes::PlanarSurfaceType':
        bb = face.boundingBox
        mid_y = (bb.minPoint.y + bb.maxPoint.y) / 2
        if abs(mid_y) < 0.1 and bb.minPoint.z < 0.1 and abs(bb.maxPoint.z - 13.0) < 0.1:
            face_at_y0 = face
            break

if face_at_y0:
    cyl_count = 0
    for edge in face_at_y0.edges:
        egeo = edge.geometry
        if hasattr(egeo, 'surfaceType') and str(egeo.surfaceType) == 'adsk::core::SurfaceTypes::CylinderSurfaceType':
            cyl_count += 1
    print(f"3. Baffle face at Y=0 found: {cyl_count} cylindrical edges (holes)")
else:
    print(f"3. ERROR: No face at Y=0 found on body")

print(f"4. Baffle cuts complete")

# ============================================================
# STEP 4: Flange rebates + mounting holes on front face
# ============================================================
# Rebate circles create annular profiles. Need to offset plane slightly.
pR = planes.createInput()
pR.setByOffset(root.xZConstructionPlane, ValueInput.createByReal(-0.02))
rebatePlane = planes.add(pR)

rebateSketch = sketches.add(rebatePlane)
rebateSketch.name = "RebatesAndScrews"
rebateSketch.isComputeDeferred = True
rCircles = rebateSketch.sketchCurves.sketchCircles

# W3 flange OD ring: outer r=4.605cm (creates ring with existing cutout)
rCircles.addByCenterRadius(Point3D.create(7.0, -7.0, 0), 4.605)
# RS75 flange OD ring
rCircles.addByCenterRadius(Point3D.create(15.3, -7.0, 0), 3.59)

# W3 mounting holes (4 at 42.86mm bolt circle, 45/135/225/315°)
w3_bolt_r = 4.286
for deg in [45, 135, 225, 315]:
    rad = math.radians(deg)
    hx = 7.0 + w3_bolt_r * math.cos(rad)
    hz = 7.0 + w3_bolt_r * math.sin(rad)
    rCircles.addByCenterRadius(Point3D.create(hx, -hz, 0), 0.225)

# RS75 mounting holes (4 at 32mm bolt circle)
rs75_bolt_r = 3.2
for deg in [45, 135, 225, 315]:
    rad = math.radians(deg)
    hx = 15.3 + rs75_bolt_r * math.cos(rad)
    hz = 7.0 + rs75_bolt_r * math.sin(rad)
    rCircles.addByCenterRadius(Point3D.create(hx, -hz, 0), 0.21)

rebateSketch.isComputeDeferred = False

rp_count = rebateSketch.profiles.count
for i in range(rp_count):
    prof = rebateSketch.profiles.item(i)
    area = prof.areaProperties().area
    if 23 < area < 26:
        depth = 0.4  # W3 rebate 4mm
    elif 17 < area < 20:
        depth = 0.35  # RS75 rebate 3.5mm
    else:
        depth = 0.8  # screw holes through full baffle
    ext4 = extrudes.createInput(prof, adsk.fusion.FeatureOperations.CutFeatureOperation)
    ext4.setDistanceExtent(False, ValueInput.createByReal(depth))
    ext4.participantBodies = [boxBody]
    feat = extrudes.add(ext4)
    if feat:
        pass  # print(f"  Rebate/screw {i} succeeded")

print(f"5. Rebates and screws: {rp_count} profiles processed")

# ============================================================
# STEP 5: Center rib on interior floor
# ============================================================
# Rib: X=9.85→10.15 (3mm thick), Y=1.3→11.0 (full height), at Z=0.5
rSk = sketches.add(floorPlane)  # reuse floorPlane at Z=0.5
rSk.name = "CenterRib"
rLines = rSk.sketchCurves.sketchLines
rLines.addTwoPointRectangle(Point3D.create(9.85, 1.3, 0), Point3D.create(10.15, 11.0, 0))
r_prof = None
for i in range(rSk.profiles.count):
    p = rSk.profiles.item(i)
    if 2.8 < p.areaProperties().area < 3.0:
        r_prof = p
        break

if r_prof:
    ext5 = extrudes.createInput(r_prof, adsk.fusion.FeatureOperations.JoinFeatureOperation)
    ext5.setDistanceExtent(False, ValueInput.createByReal(12.0))
    ext5.participantBodies = [boxBody]
    ribFeat = extrudes.add(ext5)
    print(f"6. Center rib created")

# ============================================================
# STEP 6: Amp standoffs on interior floor (Z=0.5cm)
# ============================================================
standoffSketch = sketches.add(floorPlane)
standoffSketch.name = "StandoffBases"
sCircles = standoffSketch.sketchCurves.sketchCircles

# Standoff positions: 9.2x6.4cm pattern centered on (6.5, 7.4)
sx, sy = 6.5, 7.4
pw, pd = 4.6, 3.2
positions = [(sx-pw, sy-pd), (sx+pw, sy-pd), (sx-pw, sy+pd), (sx+pw, sy+pd)]
for (px, py) in positions:
    sCircles.addByCenterRadius(Point3D.create(px, py, 0), 0.4)  # 8mm diameter base

s_prof_count = standoffSketch.profiles.count
standoff_bodies = []
for i in range(s_prof_count):
    prof = standoffSketch.profiles.item(i)
    ext6 = extrudes.createInput(prof, adsk.fusion.FeatureOperations.JoinFeatureOperation)
    ext6.setDistanceExtent(False, ValueInput.createByReal(0.6))  # 6mm tall
    ext6.participantBodies = [boxBody]
    feat = extrudes.add(ext6)
    if feat:
        for b in feat.bodies:
            standoff_bodies.append(b)
            b.name = f"Standoff_{b.index}"
    print(f"  Standoff base {i} created")

# Standoff insert holes: offset XY plane at Z=1.1cm (0.5+0.6)
pS = planes.createInput()
pS.setByOffset(root.xYConstructionPlane, ValueInput.createByReal(1.1))
standoffTopPlane = planes.add(pS)

shSketch = sketches.add(standoffTopPlane)
shSketch.name = "StandoffInsertHoles"
shCircles = shSketch.sketchCurves.sketchCircles
for (px, py) in positions:
    shCircles.addByCenterRadius(Point3D.create(px, py, 0), 0.21)  # 4.2mm M3 hole

sh_prof_count = shSketch.profiles.count
sh_count = 0
for i in range(sh_prof_count):
    prof = shSketch.profiles.item(i)
    ext7 = extrudes.createInput(prof, adsk.fusion.FeatureOperations.CutFeatureOperation)
    ext7.setDistanceExtent(False, ValueInput.createByReal(-0.5))  # 5mm deep
    if standoff_bodies:
        ext7.participantBodies = [boxBody]  # cut through joined standoffs
    feat = extrudes.add(ext7)
    if feat:
        sh_count += 1
print(f"7. Standoff insert holes: {sh_count} cut")

# ============================================================
# STEP 7: Back panel (separate body)
# ============================================================
bpSketch = sketches.add(root.xYConstructionPlane)
bpSketch.name = "BackPanelSketch"
bpLines = bpSketch.sketchCurves.sketchLines
bpLines.addTwoPointRectangle(Point3D.create(0, 12.0, 0), Point3D.create(20.0, 12.5, 0))
bp_prof = bpSketch.profiles.item(0)
ext8 = extrudes.createInput(bp_prof, adsk.fusion.FeatureOperations.NewBodyFeatureOperation)
ext8.setDistanceExtent(False, ValueInput.createByReal(13.0))
backPanelFeat = extrudes.add(ext8)
for b in backPanelFeat.bodies:
    b.name = "Back_Panel"
backPanel = None
for b in backPanelFeat.bodies:
    backPanel = b
print(f"8. Back panel created: {backPanel.name}")

# Back panel screw holes (6 bosses) + power jack
pBP = planes.createInput()
pBP.setByOffset(root.xZConstructionPlane, ValueInput.createByReal(12.5))
bpHolePlane = planes.add(pBP)

bpHoleSketch = sketches.add(bpHolePlane)
bpHoleSketch.name = "BackPanelHoles"
bpHoleSketch.isComputeDeferred = True
bpHCircles = bpHoleSketch.sketchCurves.sketchCircles

# Boss positions (X, -Z on XZ plane)
for (bx, bz) in [(1.2, -1.2), (10, -1.2), (18.8, -1.2), (1.2, -11.8), (10, -11.8), (18.8, -11.8)]:
    bpHCircles.addByCenterRadius(Point3D.create(bx, bz, 0), 0.16)  # 3.2mm clearance
bpHCircles.addByCenterRadius(Point3D.create(17.5, -2.5, 0), 0.325)  # 6.5mm power jack
bpHoleSketch.isComputeDeferred = False

bp_hole_count = bpHoleSketch.profiles.count
for i in range(bp_hole_count):
    prof = bpHoleSketch.profiles.item(i)
    ext9 = extrudes.createInput(prof, adsk.fusion.FeatureOperations.CutFeatureOperation)
    ext9.setDistanceExtent(False, ValueInput.createByReal(0.5))  # 5mm thickness
    ext9.participantBodies = [backPanel]
    feat = extrudes.add(ext9)
print(f"9. Back panel holes: {bp_hole_count} cut")

# ============================================================
# STEP 8: Port tube (separate body)
# ============================================================
pPT = planes.createInput()
pPT.setByOffset(root.xZConstructionPlane, ValueInput.createByReal(0))  # XZ plane
portSketch = sketches.add(root.xZConstructionPlane)
portSketch.name = "PortTube"
portCircles = portSketch.sketchCurves.sketchCircles
# Center at X=11.2, Z=2.3 → sketch (11.2, -2.3)
portCircles.addByCenterRadius(Point3D.create(11.2, -2.3, 0), 1.45)
portCircles.addByCenterRadius(Point3D.create(11.2, -2.3, 0), 1.25)

port_prof = None
for i in range(portSketch.profiles.count):
    p = portSketch.profiles.item(i)
    area = p.areaProperties().area
    if 1.6 < area < 1.8:
        port_prof = p
        print(f"10. Port ring profile found at index {i} (area={area:.4f})")

if port_prof:
    ext10 = extrudes.createInput(port_prof, adsk.fusion.FeatureOperations.NewBodyFeatureOperation)
    ext10.setDistanceExtent(False, ValueInput.createByReal(8.3))  # 83mm long
    portFeat = extrudes.add(ext10)
    for b in portFeat.bodies:
        b.name = "Port_Tube"
    print(f"11. Port tube created")

# ============================================================
# STEP 9: Back panel mounting bosses (inside box, at Y=12 face)
# ============================================================
# These attach to the interior back face (Y=12), protruding inward (-Y)
pBoss = planes.createInput()
pBoss.setByOffset(root.xZConstructionPlane, ValueInput.createByReal(12.0))
bossPlane = planes.add(pBoss)

bossSketch = sketches.add(bossPlane)
bossSketch.name = "InnerBosses"
bossSketch.isComputeDeferred = True
bossCircles = bossSketch.sketchCurves.sketchCircles
for (bx, bz) in [(1.2, -1.2), (10, -1.2), (18.8, -1.2), (1.2, -11.8), (10, -11.8), (18.8, -11.8)]:
    bossCircles.addByCenterRadius(Point3D.create(bx, bz, 0), 0.4)  # 8mm diameter
bossSketch.isComputeDeferred = False

boss_prof_count = bossSketch.profiles.count
boss_count = 0
for i in range(boss_prof_count):
    prof = bossSketch.profiles.item(i)
    ext11 = extrudes.createInput(prof, adsk.fusion.FeatureOperations.JoinFeatureOperation)
    ext11.setDistanceExtent(False, ValueInput.createByReal(-1.2))  # -Y = inward
    ext11.participantBodies = [boxBody]
    feat = extrudes.add(ext11)
    if feat:
        boss_count += 1
print(f"12. Inner bosses: {boss_count} joined")

# Boss insert holes (4.2mm, from back face)
pIns = planes.createInput()
pIns.setByOffset(root.xZConstructionPlane, ValueInput.createByReal(12.5))
insPlane = planes.add(pIns)

insSketch = sketches.add(insPlane)
insSketch.name = "BossInsertHoles"
insCircles = insSketch.sketchCurves.sketchCircles
for (bx, bz) in [(1.2, -1.2), (10, -1.2), (18.8, -1.2), (1.2, -11.8), (10, -11.8), (18.8, -11.8)]:
    insCircles.addByCenterRadius(Point3D.create(bx, bz, 0), 0.21)

ins_prof_count = insSketch.profiles.count
ins_count = 0
for i in range(ins_prof_count):
    prof = insSketch.profiles.item(i)
    ext12 = extrudes.createInput(prof, adsk.fusion.FeatureOperations.CutFeatureOperation)
    ext12.setDistanceExtent(False, ValueInput.createByReal(-1.2))  # through boss depth
    ext12.participantBodies = [boxBody]
    feat = extrudes.add(ext12)
    if feat:
        ins_count += 1
print(f"13. Boss insert holes: {ins_count} cut")

# ============================================================
# STEP 10: External fillets
# ============================================================
fillets = root.features.filletFeatures
filletInput = fillets.createInput()

# Find vertical corner edges and top edges
vert_edges = []
top_edges = []
for i in range(boxBody.edges.count):
    edge = boxBody.edges.item(i)
    sg = edge.geometry
    if hasattr(sg, 'startPoint') and hasattr(sg, 'endPoint'):
        dx = abs(sg.endPoint.x - sg.startPoint.x)
        dy = abs(sg.endPoint.y - sg.startPoint.y)
        dz = abs(sg.endPoint.z - sg.startPoint.z)
        mx = (sg.startPoint.x + sg.endPoint.x) / 2
        my = (sg.startPoint.y + sg.endPoint.y) / 2
        mz = (sg.startPoint.z + sg.endPoint.z) / 2
        
        if dz > 0.1 and dx < 0.1 and dy < 0.1:
            if (abs(mx) < 0.1 or abs(mx - 20) < 0.1) and (abs(my) < 0.1 or abs(my - 12) < 0.1):
                vert_edges.append(i)
        elif dz < 0.1 and abs(mz - 13) < 0.1:
            if (abs(mx) < 0.1 or abs(mx - 20) < 0.1 or abs(my) < 0.1 or abs(my - 12) < 0.1):
                top_edges.append(i)

all_fillet_edges = vert_edges + top_edges
edges = adsk.core.ObjectCollection.create()
for i in all_fillet_edges:
    edges.add(boxBody.edges.item(i))

filletInput.addConstantRadiusEdgeSet(edges, ValueInput.createByReal(0.3), True)
filFeat = fillets.add(filletInput)
print(f"14. Fillets: {len(all_fillet_edges)} edges at 3mm")

# ============================================================
# FINAL SUMMARY
# ============================================================
print(f"\n=== FINAL STATE ===")
print(f"Bodies:")
for body in root.bRepBodies:
    print(f"  {body.name}")

planes = root.constructionPlanes

boxSketch = sketches.add(root.xYConstructionPlane)

lines = boxSketch.sketchCurves.sketchLines
lines.addTwoPointRectangle(Point3D.create(0, 0, 0), Point3D.create(20, 12, 0))

boxFeat = extrudes.add(ext1)
boxBody = None
for b in boxFeat.bodies:
    b.name = "Enclosure_Shell"
    boxBody = b
print("1. Box created")

# === STEP 2: Hollow interior ===
# Offset XY at Z=0.5
pIn = planes.createInput()
pIn.setByOffset(root.xYConstructionPlane, ValueInput.createByReal(0.5))
floorPlane = planes.add(pIn)

intSketch = sketches.add(floorPlane)
intSketch.name = "InteriorCut"
iLines = intSketch.sketchCurves.sketchLines
iLines.addTwoPointRectangle(Point3D.create(0.5, 0.8, 0), Point3D.create(19.5, 11.5, 0))
int_prof = intSketch.profiles.item(0)
ext2 = extrudes.createInput(int_prof, FeatureOperations.CutFeatureOperation)
ext2.setDistanceExtent(False, ValueInput.createByReal(12.0))
ext2.participantBodies = [boxBody]
extrudes.add(ext2)
print("2. Interior hollowed")

# === STEP 3: Baffle cutouts using FRONT FACE sketch ===
# Create a sketch directly on the front face (Y=0 planar face of the box)
# First find the front face
frontFace = None
for face in boxBody.faces:
    geo = face.geometry
    if hasattr(geo, 'surfaceType') and str(geo.surfaceType) == 'adsk::core::SurfaceTypes::PlanarSurfaceType':
        bb = face.boundingBox
        mid_y = (bb.minPoint.y + bb.maxPoint.y) / 2
        if abs(mid_y) < 0.1:
            frontFace = face
            break

if frontFace:
    print(f"3. Front face found at Y=0")
    
    # Sketch ON the face
    baffleSketch = sketches.add(frontFace)
    baffleSketch.name = "BaffleCuts"
    baffleSketch.isComputeDeferred = True
    
    # On a face sketch, coords are in the face's local UV space
    # For a face at Y=0 (in XY plane orientation), local U maps to X, local V maps to Z
    # So point (x, z) in sketch maps to world (x, 0, z)
    
    bCircles = baffleSketch.sketchCurves.sketchCircles
    bCircles.addByCenterRadius(Point3D.create(7.0, 7.0, 0), 3.71)
    bCircles.addByCenterRadius(Point3D.create(15.3, 7.0, 0), 2.705)
    bCircles.addByCenterRadius(Point3D.create(11.2, 2.3, 0), 1.25)
    bCircles.addByCenterRadius(Point3D.create(14.2, 2.2, 0), 0.35)
    bCircles.addByCenterRadius(Point3D.create(16.0, 2.2, 0), 0.35)
    bCircles.addByCenterRadius(Point3D.create(17.8, 2.2, 0), 0.35)
    bCircles.addByCenterRadius(Point3D.create(12.7, 2.2, 0), 0.25)
    
    baffleSketch.isComputeDeferred = False

import math

root = rootComp
sketches = root.sketches
planes = root.constructionPlanes
extrudes = root.features.extrudeFeatures

boxBody = root.bRepBodies.itemByName("Enclosure_Shell")
print(f"Box volume: {boxBody.areaProperties().volume:.4f}")

# === STEP: Cutouts using ThroughAll from front face ===
# The issue is that cutting from XZ offset plane doesn't penetrate.
# Solution: Create cut profiles on the XY plane (Z=0) which IS the front face plane.
# But we need to be slightly offset to avoid coincident plane issues.

# Create a plane slightly in front of Y=0

pFront.setByOffset(root.xZConstructionPlane, adsk.core.ValueInput.createByReal(-0.05))  # -0.05cm = -0.5mm
frontPlane = planes.add(pFront)

# Sketch circles on this plane at the correct positions
# XZ plane: Point3D.create(x, -z_world, 0) maps to world (x, 0, z_world)
baffleSketch = sketches.add(frontPlane)
baffleSketch.name = "BaffleHoles_CutBodies"
baffleSketch.isComputeDeferred = True
bCircles = baffleSketch.sketchCurves.sketchCircles

# All cutouts with their center positions (world X, world Z)
cuts = [
    (7.0, 7.0, 3.71),    # W3-2141
    (15.3, 7.0, 2.705),  # RS75-4
    (11.2, 2.3, 1.25),   # Port
    (14.2, 2.2, 0.35),   # POT1
    (16.0, 2.2, 0.35),   # POT2
    (17.8, 2.2, 0.35),   # POT3
    (12.7, 2.2, 0.25),   # LED
]

for (cx, cz, r) in cuts:
    bCircles.addByCenterRadius(Point3D.create(cx, -cz, 0), r)

baffleSketch.isComputeDeferred = False

# Create solid cylinders (short) that extend into the box from the front
# These will be separate bodies that intersect the box
for i in range(baffleSketch.profiles.count):
    prof = baffleSketch.profiles.item(i)
    area = prof.areaProperties().area
    
    # Extrude as new body through the baffle thickness
    ext = extrudes.createInput(prof, FeatureOperations.NewBodyFeatureOperation)
    ext.setDistanceExtent(False, ValueInput.createByReal(0.8))  # 8mm = baffle thickness
    cutFeat = extrudes.add(ext)
    if cutFeat:
        for b in cutFeat.bodies:
            b.name = f"BaffleCut_{i}"
        print(f"Cut body {i} (r={math.sqrt(area/math.pi):.3f}) created")

# Now boolean cut these cut-bodies from the main body
print("\n=== Performing boolean cuts ===")
cut_bodies = []
for body in root.bRepBodies:
    if body.name.startswith("BaffleCut"):
        cut_bodies.append(body)
        print(f"  Will cut with {body.name}")

# Now do the boolean operations
cut_count = 0
for cb in cut_bodies:
    # Use the cut body to cut the main body
    # In Fusion 360, we can use the cut body itself with a cut extrusion
    # Or we can use the CutFeatureOperation with participant bodies
    ext = extrudes.createInput(cb.bRepBodies[0] if hasattr(cb, 'bRepBodies') else cb, FeatureOperations.CutFeatureOperation)
    # Use ThroughAll in +Y direction
    ext.setAllExtent(adsk.fusion.ThroughAllExtentDefinition.create(), adsk.fusion.ExtentDirections.PositiveExtentDirection)
    ext.participantBodies = [boxBody]
    
    feat = extrudes.add(ext)
    if feat:
        cut_count += 1
        # Delete the cut body after use
        cb.deleteMe()

print(f"Boolean cuts performed: {cut_count}")

# Check for cylindrical edges now
cyl = 0
for face in boxBody.faces:
    for e in face.edges:
        eg = e.geometry
        if hasattr(eg, 'surfaceType') and 'Cylinder' in str(eg.surfaceType):
            cyl += 1
            print(f"  Cyl edge: r={eg.radius:.3f} at ({eg.origin.x:.3f}, {eg.origin.y:.3f}, {eg.origin.z:.3f})")
print(f"Total cylindrical edges: {cyl}")
